title: Hundreds of flight cancellations leave passengers frustrated and stranded
subhed: The war in the Middle East has caused the price of jet fuel to double in just a few weeks. Many airlines, especially low-cost carriers, have canceled flights with low occupancy or low profitability. Passenger rights are protected, but within certain limits.

First trial opens over Paris after-school sexual abuse scandal
subhed: An activity leader was to appear on Tuesday, May 5, before the Paris criminal court. He is accused of sexually harassing nine primary school girls and sexually assaulting three of them.

title: Sarkozy's counter-offensive leaves judges unconvinced at Libyan funding trial
subhed: The former French president once again tried on Monday to prove that the sums paid by Libyans were not used to fund his 2007 presidential campaign.

title: How a dream cruise turned into a health nightmare
subhed: Hantavirus is responsible for the death of at least one of the three passengers who died this past month. While another three people on board are showing symptoms of acute respiratory distress, the luxury cruise ship, which Cape Verdean authorities have banned from docking, could head for the Canary Islands.

title: Military personnel at French presidency suspected of compromising classified documents
subhed: Prosecutors are investigating several members of the military and civilians suspected of setting up a private company to sell classified information and weapons of war. Six suspects have been charged.

subhed: Six months ahead of the US midterm elections, the political momentum has turned sharply against President Donald Trump.

title: Paul Seixas, French cycling prodigy, to participate in first Tour de France
subhed: The 19-year-old cyclist is set to take part in the three-week race this summer. Since the start of the season, his performances have made him a serious contender for the podium.

title: Jean-Luc Mélenchon announces fourth presidential run
subhed: Despite the emergence of several figures within La France Insoumise and his promise to step aside after the 2022 election, in which he gathered 21.95% of the vote, the populist leader announced his candidacy on Sunday.
